meta.name: Microsoft Power Platform Fundamentals (PL-900)
meta.description: Foundational Microsoft Power Platform concepts for the PL-900 certification exam.

cards.definition: Power Platform is a suite of low-code/no-code tools for building business solutions: Power Apps (custom apps), Power Automate (workflow automation), Power BI (data analytics and visualisation), Power Pages (external-facing websites), and Copilot Studio (AI chatbots). All tools share Microsoft Dataverse as a common data store and integrate natively with Microsoft 365 and Dynamics 365.
cards.hint: Apps + Automation + Analytics + Websites + Chatbots — all low-code, all connected.

cards.definition: A low-code environment for building custom business applications without deep programming knowledge. Canvas Apps give full UI control — you design each screen by dragging and dropping. Model-driven Apps are data-first — built on Dataverse tables with auto-generated UI. Power Apps can connect to hundreds of data sources via connectors and run on web, iOS, and Android.
cards.hint: Canvas = blank canvas, you design everything. Model-driven = Dataverse tables drive the UI.

cards.definition: A workflow automation service that connects apps and services to automate repetitive tasks. Flows are triggered by events (new email, form submission, schedule) and run actions (send notification, create record, approve). Flow types: Cloud flows (automated, instant, scheduled), Desktop flows (robotic process automation — RPA for legacy systems), and Business process flows (guide users through multi-step processes).
cards.hint: If this happens → do that. Automate approvals, notifications, and data movement between apps.

cards.definition: Microsoft's self-service business intelligence tool for creating interactive reports and dashboards. Data is loaded and transformed using Power Query (M language), modelled with DAX (Data Analysis Expressions) measures, and visualised with charts, maps, and tables. Reports are published to the Power BI Service for sharing. Power BI Desktop = authoring; Power BI Service = cloud sharing; Power BI Mobile = on the go.
cards.hint: Connect data → model it → visualise it → share it. All self-service, no SQL needed.

cards.definition: A cloud-based, low-code data platform that provides secure, scalable storage for business data used by Power Platform apps. Data is organised into tables (like database tables) with columns, relationships, and built-in business logic (business rules, calculated columns, rollups). Includes out-of-the-box tables for common business data. All Model-driven Apps and Copilot Studio bots are built on Dataverse.
cards.hint: The shared, secure database that all Power Platform tools can store and read data from.

cards.definition: Connectors are pre-built bridges between Power Platform and external services (Microsoft 365, SharePoint, Dynamics 365, Salesforce, Twitter, SQL Server, etc.). Standard connectors are included in most licences. Premium connectors (e.g., Salesforce, SQL Server, HTTP) require a higher licence tier. Custom connectors can be built for any REST API. Data Gateway enables connectors to reach on-premises data sources.
cards.hint: Plug-in adapters that let Power Platform talk to over 1,000 different apps and services.

cards.definition: A low-code platform for building secure, data-driven external-facing websites accessible by anonymous or authenticated external users (customers, partners, community members). Built on Dataverse — pages display and collect Dataverse data. Previously called Power Apps Portals. Includes built-in templates, liquid templating language, and role-based access control for external users.
cards.hint: Build a public-facing website connected to your Dataverse data, without writing web code.

cards.definition: A low-code platform for building AI-powered chatbots (copilots) without writing code. Bots are built with topics (conversation branches triggered by user intents), actions (Power Automate flows for data operations), and entities (data extraction from user messages). Bots can be published to Teams, websites, and other channels. Generative AI answers powered by Azure OpenAI can be added with minimal configuration.
cards.hint: Build a smart chatbot by drawing conversation branches — no coding required.

cards.definition: An environment is a space to store, manage, and share your Power Platform apps, flows, and Dataverse data. Every organisation has a default environment. Additional environments (Development, Test, Production) are used for application lifecycle management (ALM). Environments can be restricted to specific regions and have their own security roles and data policies (DLP policies).
cards.hint: Separate containers for Dev, Test, and Production — each with its own apps and data.

cards.term: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Policies in Power Platform
cards.definition: DLP policies in Power Platform control which connectors can be used together in a single flow or app, preventing sensitive business data from being shared with external services unintentionally. Connectors are classified as Business (internal, trusted) or Non-Business (external, consumer). A DLP policy can prevent a Business connector (e.g., SharePoint) from mixing with a Non-Business connector (e.g., Twitter).
cards.hint: Rules that stop apps from accidentally sending SharePoint data to a personal Twitter account.

cards.definition: Canvas Apps give full control over layout and design — ideal for task-focused mobile or tablet apps. Model-Driven Apps are data-first, automatically generating UI based on Dataverse tables, forms, and views. Model-Driven Apps support complex business logic, security roles, and dashboards.
cards.hint: Canvas = design-first. Model-Driven = data-first.

cards.definition: Cloud flows automate tasks across cloud services. Types: Automated (triggered by an event), Instant (triggered manually), and Scheduled (run on a timer). Common uses: notifications, approvals, data sync, and document processing.
cards.hint: Automate tasks across apps with event-based or scheduled flows.

cards.definition: Robotic Process Automation (RPA) automates legacy systems and desktop tasks by recording clicks, keystrokes, and UI interactions. Useful for systems without APIs. Runs attended (user present) or unattended (fully automated).
cards.hint: Automate old desktop apps that don't have connectors.

cards.definition: Guide users through multi-step business processes such as sales qualification or case resolution. BPFs enforce consistency, required steps, and stage transitions. Used primarily in Model-Driven Apps.
cards.hint: Visual step-by-step guidance for business processes.

cards.definition: Power BI connects to hundreds of data sources: Excel, SQL Server, SharePoint, Dataverse, Azure Synapse, Salesforce, and more. Supports import mode (data loaded into Power BI) and DirectQuery (live queries to the source).
cards.hint: Connect Power BI to almost any data — cloud or on-prem.

cards.definition: Reports are multi-page, interactive visuals created in Power BI Desktop. Dashboards are single-page, pin-board style views created in the Power BI Service by pinning visuals from reports. Dashboards can combine visuals from multiple reports.
cards.hint: Reports = multi-page. Dashboards = one-page summary.

cards.definition: Security roles control access to tables, rows, and columns. Permissions include Create, Read, Write, Delete, Append, and Share. Access levels include User, Business Unit, Parent-Child, and Organization.
cards.hint: Roles define what users can do with Dataverse data.

cards.definition: Dataverse includes Standard Tables (common business data), Custom Tables (user-defined), Activity Tables (emails, calls, tasks), and Virtual Tables (external data accessed in real time).
cards.hint: Standard, custom, activity, and virtual tables.

cards.definition: Reusable configuration values (URLs, IDs, connection settings) used across apps and flows. Essential for ALM when moving solutions between Dev, Test, and Production environments.
cards.hint: Store settings once — reuse everywhere.

cards.definition: Solutions package apps, flows, tables, and components for deployment across environments. Managed solutions are locked for production; unmanaged solutions are editable for development.
cards.hint: The packaging system for moving apps between environments.

cards.definition: A low-code AI service that adds intelligence to apps and flows. Prebuilt models include sentiment analysis, key phrase extraction, prediction, form processing, object detection, and receipt scanning. Custom models can be trained using your own data.
cards.hint: Add AI to apps without writing machine learning code.

cards.definition: Licensing includes per-app plans (use specific apps), per-user plans (unlimited apps), and pay-as-you-go via Azure. Premium connectors, Dataverse, and RPA require higher-tier licenses.
cards.hint: Premium connectors and Dataverse need premium licenses.

cards.definition: The central portal for managing environments, DLP policies, analytics, capacity, and security roles. Admins can monitor app usage, flow runs, and Dataverse storage.
cards.hint: The control center for managing the entire Power Platform.

cards.definition: A gateway that allows Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI to securely access on-premises data sources like SQL Server, SharePoint Server, and file shares.
cards.hint: Secure bridge between cloud apps and on-prem data.

cards.definition: A low-code formula language used in Canvas Apps. Similar to Excel formulas, used for logic, calculations, and UI behavior. Example: `If(IsBlank(TextInput1.Text), "Missing", "OK")`.
cards.hint: Excel-style formulas for building app logic.

cards.definition: Data modeling includes relationships, measures (DAX), calculated columns, and star schema design. Good modeling improves performance and report clarity.
cards.hint: Clean models = faster, clearer Power BI reports.

cards.definition: A built-in approval system for workflows. Supports sequential, parallel, and custom approval processes. Approvals can be completed in Teams, Outlook, or the Power Automate app.
cards.hint: Automate approval workflows across apps.

cards.definition: Power Pages supports anonymous access, local authentication, Azure AD B2C, Microsoft Entra ID, and external identity providers. Access is controlled through Dataverse table permissions.
cards.hint: Secure your website with multiple identity options.

cards.definition: Copilot can generate app screens, Dataverse tables, and formulas from natural language prompts. Helps users build apps faster by suggesting UI elements and data structures.
cards.hint: Describe your app — Copilot builds it.

cards.definition: Governance includes environment strategy, DLP policies, security roles, monitoring, and ALM. Ensures apps and flows are secure, compliant, and scalable across the organization.
cards.hint: Rules and structure that keep Power Platform safe and manageable.

cards.definition: Canvas Apps can connect to hundreds of data sources using connectors. Common sources include SharePoint, Excel, SQL Server, Dataverse, and OneDrive. Data is accessed through galleries, forms, and controls using Power Fx formulas.
cards.hint: Canvas Apps pull data from connectors like SharePoint or SQL.

cards.definition: Model-Driven Apps are built from Dataverse components: tables, forms, views, charts, dashboards, and business process flows. The UI is generated automatically based on the data model.
cards.hint: Tables + forms + views = a Model-Driven App.

cards.definition: Triggers start a flow. Types include event-based triggers (new email, new record), manual triggers (button press), and scheduled triggers (run every hour). Premium triggers require premium licensing.
cards.hint: Every flow starts with a trigger.

cards.definition: Actions are the steps a flow performs after a trigger. Examples: create a Dataverse record, send an email, update a SharePoint item, call an API, or start an approval.
cards.hint: Actions are the tasks your flow performs.

cards.definition: Power Query is the ETL engine used to clean, transform, and shape data before loading it into Power BI. Uses the M language. Common transformations include filtering, merging, grouping, and pivoting.
cards.hint: Clean and transform data before modeling.

cards.definition: DAX (Data Analysis Expressions) is the formula language used to create measures and calculated columns in Power BI. Examples: SUM(), CALCULATE(), FILTER(). Enables advanced analytics and KPIs.
cards.hint: The formula language for Power BI calculations.

cards.definition: Business rules enforce logic at the data layer without code. They can set field values, show/hide fields, validate data, or lock fields. They apply across all apps using the table.
cards.hint: No-code rules that enforce data logic.

cards.definition: Calculated columns compute values using formulas (e.g., Total = Price × Quantity). Rollup columns aggregate data from related records (e.g., total revenue from opportunities).
cards.hint: Auto-calculated fields based on formulas or related data.

cards.definition: A governance framework that helps organizations manage adoption, security, monitoring, and best practices. Often implemented using the CoE Starter Kit.
cards.hint: A structured approach to governing Power Platform at scale.

cards.definition: Extracts structured data from documents such as invoices, receipts, and forms. Trained using sample documents. Outputs fields that can be used in apps and flows.
cards.hint: AI that reads documents and extracts key fields.

cards.definition: Predicts outcomes based on historical data (e.g., whether a customer will renew). Requires labeled training data stored in Dataverse.
cards.hint: Train AI to predict yes/no or numeric outcomes.

cards.definition: Web roles control what authenticated users can see or do on a Power Pages site. Roles map to Dataverse table permissions for granular access control.
cards.hint: Permissions for external users on Power Pages.

cards.definition: Liquid is a templating language used to display Dataverse data dynamically on Power Pages. Supports loops, conditions, and data queries.
cards.hint: Code-like templates for dynamic website content.

cards.definition: Flows enhanced with AI Builder or Copilot to classify text, extract data, or generate content. Useful for document automation and customer service scenarios.
cards.hint: Automation boosted with AI insights.

cards.definition: Allows developers to build custom UI components for Model-Driven Apps and Canvas Apps. Components can replace standard controls with richer experiences.
cards.hint: Build custom controls for Power Apps.

cards.definition: Restricts data visibility for users based on roles. Ensures users only see data they are authorized to view. Configured in Power BI Desktop and enforced in the Service.
cards.hint: Limit which rows of data each user can see.

cards.definition: Workspaces are collaboration areas for building and publishing reports. Sharing options include dashboards, apps, and secure sharing links. Pro licenses are required for sharing.
cards.hint: Where teams collaborate on BI content.

cards.definition: Flows can use parallel branches, scopes, and run-after conditions to handle failures gracefully. Useful for retry logic, notifications, and fallback actions.
cards.hint: Make flows resilient with run-after and error logic.

cards.definition: Admins can monitor app usage, flow runs, Dataverse storage, and performance using the Admin Center, CoE dashboards, and Azure Monitor integration.
cards.hint: Track performance, usage, and capacity across the platform.

cards.term: Power Platform ALM with GitHub/Azure DevOps
cards.definition: Solutions can be exported, versioned, and deployed using pipelines in Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions. Supports automated testing, environment promotion, and CI/CD.
cards.hint: Automate app deployment with DevOps pipelines.
